context: Tests whether the skill checks for pending complaints at the start of a session, correctly resolves them based on user responses, logs credits to the travel credits inventory when the resolution includes miles/vouchers, and uses CLOSED for no-response cases to prevent re-asking.

checklist.name: Pending complaints checked first
checklist.description: session-log.md shows that pending complaints were checked before starting intake for the new complaint — the pending check happens first, not after

checklist.name: DL891 resolved as PARTIAL
checklist.description: Records DL891 resolution as PARTIAL (or RESOLVED) — the user accepted 15K miles and a $50 voucher, which is less than the 25K miles requested

checklist.name: DL891 credits logged
checklist.description: Shows the credits-tracker.py add command (or describes logging) for the 15,000 miles AND the $50 voucher from the DL891 resolution — both should be tracked in the travel credits inventory

checklist.name: DL2044 resolved as CLOSED
checklist.description: Records DL2044 resolution as CLOSED (not PENDING, not DENIED) — the user said 'complete silence' and 'done waiting', so CLOSED is correct to prevent the skill from asking again

checklist.name: DL2044 no credits logged
checklist.description: Does NOT attempt to log credits for DL2044 since the airline never responded — there is nothing to log

checklist.name: DOT escalation suggested for DL2044
checklist.description: Suggests or notes that the DL2044 downgrade with no response after 30+ days may warrant a DOT complaint filing, since the user has given up on the airline's direct response

checklist.name: New complaint intake proceeds
checklist.description: After resolving pending complaints, proceeds to intake for the new Spirit NK412 complaint — asks relevant follow-up questions

checklist.name: Voucher expiry captured
checklist.description: The credits-tracker command for the $50 voucher includes the December 2026 expiry date that the user mentioned

checklist.name: Resolve commands shown
checklist.description: Shows or describes the complaints-bank.py resolve commands for both complaints with appropriate --resolution and --note arguments
